chapter_title: Products of the milling industry; malt; starches; inulin; wheat gluten
scope_note: Note 2(A): cereal milling products classify in Ch.11 if starch content EXCEEDS minimum (per Ewers polarimetric method) AND ash content does not exceed maximum — thresholds vary by cereal. Failing these → 23.02 (bran/residues for feed). Exception: cereal germs always → 11.04. Note 2(B): further classified as flour (11.01/11.02) if ≥specified % passes through metal wire sieve, else groats/meal (11.03/11.04). Note 3: 'groats and meal' (11.03) = products where ≥95% passes through 2mm sieve (maize) or 1.25mm sieve (other cereals).

inclusions.description: Wheat or meslin flour: plain white wheat flour (from common wheat), wholemeal wheat flour, semolina of common wheat (if fine enough — but note semolina/coarse grind → 11.03). Meslin flour. Durum wheat flour — may go to 11.01 or 11.03 depending on particle size

Cereal flours other than wheat or meslin: rye flour, maize/corn flour, rice flour, barley flour, oat flour, buckwheat flour, millet flour, sorghum flour, quinoa flour. Each at fine flour particle size

Cereal groats, meal and pellets: wheat semolina (durum — for pasta), wheat middlings, corn/maize grits (for polenta), cornmeal, rye groats, barley grits, oat groats, sorghum grits. Note 3: ≥95% must pass through 2mm sieve (maize) or 1.25mm (others). Also PELLETS of cereal flour (bound and extruded)

Cereal grains otherwise worked (e.g., hulled, rolled, flaked, pearled, sliced or kibbled), except rice of heading 10.06: rolled oats (oat flakes), flaked barley, puffed wheat (if not ready-to-eat cereal), pearl barley, kibbled oats, rolled rye, cracked/kibbled wheat. CEREAL GERMS (whole, rolled, flaked or ground) → always 11.04 regardless of starch/ash content

Flour, meal, powder, flakes, granules and pellets of potatoes: potato flour, potato starch flour, mashed potato powder (instant mashed potato flakes/granules), potato flakes, potato pellets. Note: pure potato STARCH → 11.08

Flour, meal and powder of the dried leguminous vegetables of heading 07.13, of sago or of roots or tubers of heading 07.14; flour, meal and powder of the products of Chapter 8: chickpea flour/besan (garbanzo flour), lentil flour, pea flour, cassava/manioc flour, sweet potato flour, chestnut flour, coconut flour, banana flour, plantain flour

Malt, whether or not roasted: barley malt (malted barley — germinated and kiln-dried), wheat malt, rye malt, other cereal malt. Roasted malt (for dark beer/stout) → 11.07 (unless sold as coffee substitute → 09.01 or 21.01). Malt extract → 19.01

Starches; inulin: wheat starch (11.08.11), corn/maize starch (11.08.12), potato starch (11.08.13), cassava/tapioca starch (11.08.14), rice starch (11.08.19), other starches, inulin (from chicory root, Jerusalem artichoke — a fructooligosaccharide polymer)
Wheat gluten, whether or not dried: vital wheat gluten (protein isolated from wheat, used as dough improver, meat analogue, animal feed). Includes both fresh (wet) and dried (vital) gluten

exclusions.condition: Prepared flours, groats, meals or starches with added flavouring, sugar, or other food ingredients (beyond simple milling)
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 19.01 (malted extracts, flour/starch/malt preparations for food). Self-raising flour (with baking powder added) → 19.01. Pre-mix cake flour (with sugar, fat, baking powder) → 19.01. Baby cereal preparations (flour + vitamins + milk) → 19.01. Plain flour without additions → 11.01/11.02

exclusions.condition: Corn flakes, puffed rice, crispy rice cereal, breakfast cereals (expanded/extruded from cereals)
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 19.04 (prepared foods obtained by swelling or roasting of cereals). Corn flakes, Rice Krispies, puffed wheat → 19.04. Muesli/granola with baked oats → 19.04. The key threshold: if the cereal has been EXPANDED/POPPED/TOASTED in a way that changes the starch structure → 19.04. Rolled/flaked but not baked → 11.04

exclusions.condition: Cereal milling residues and by-products failing the starch/ash test (bran, sharps, pollard, screenings)
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 23.02 (bran, sharps and other residues from milling cereals/legumes) — when the starch content is too low and/or ash content too high for Ch.11 → cereal milling by-product → 23.02 (wheat bran, rice bran, wheat pollard, oat bran). Exception: cereal GERMS always → 11.04 regardless of starch/ash (Note 2A exception)

exclusions.condition: Roasted malt put up as coffee substitute (chicory-malt blend, barley coffee)
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 09.01 (if containing actual coffee) or 21.01 (coffee substitutes/extracts). Plain roasted malt → 11.07. Roasted malted barley specifically SOLD/PRESENTED as coffee substitute → 21.01

exclusions.condition: Starches with characteristics of perfumery, cosmetics or toilet preparations (talc with starch, body powder)
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 33 — Note 1(f). Talcum powder with starch → 33.04. Body dusting powder → 33.04. Pharmaceutical starches (excipients) → Ch.30

classification_rules.rule: Note 2 — Starch/ash content test determines Ch.11 vs Chapter 23 for milling products
classification_rules.description: The fundamental test for classifying cereal milling products between Ch.11 and Ch.23 is the DOUBLE CRITERION of Note 2(A): (1) STARCH CONTENT must EXCEED the minimum threshold for each cereal — tested by Ewers polarimetric method. (2) ASH CONTENT must NOT EXCEED the maximum threshold — indicates mineral/bran contamination. Products PASSING BOTH TESTS → Ch.11 (11.01–11.04). Products FAILING EITHER TEST → 23.02 (bran, residues for animal feed). EXCEPTION: CEREAL GERMS always → 11.04 regardless of test results. This Note prevents high-bran products from being classified as flour.

classification_rules.rule: Note 2(B) — Particle size determines flour (11.01/11.02) vs groats/meal (11.03) vs flaked/other (11.04)
classification_rules.description: Once a cereal milling product passes the starch/ash test, particle size determines the heading: FLOUR (11.01/11.02) = fine ground, passes through specified sieve (generally 315–500 micron for wheat flour). GROATS/MEAL (11.03) = coarser fragments — Note 3: ≥95% passes through 2mm sieve (maize products e.g. corn grits, polenta) or 1.25mm sieve (other cereal groats). FLAKED/ROLLED/HULLED/PEARLED grain (11.04) = grain mechanically processed but not ground to flour or meal (rolled oats are recognisably oat-like; pearl barley retains grain shape but pearled). PELLETS of flour/meal (agglomerated) → 11.03.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 11.05 (potato products) vs 11.08 (potato starch): the starch purity distinction
classification_rules.description: Potato products in Ch.11 split by PURITY: 11.05 = POTATO FLOUR, MEAL, POWDER, FLAKES, GRANULES, PELLETS — these are dried potato products that include the whole potato matrix (protein, fibre, minerals, plus starch). Instant mashed potato (dehydrated mashed potato flakes) → 11.05. 11.08 = PURE POTATO STARCH (isolated by washing away the non-starch components — wet extraction, centrifugation, drying) — white fine powder, essentially pure starch (amylose + amylopectin). The test: is it the whole potato dried (→ 11.05) or is it extracted pure starch (→ 11.08)? Tapioca starch (from cassava) → 11.08.14. Cassava FLOUR (whole cassava dried) → 11.06.

classification_rules.rule: Inulin (11.08) vs chicory root (12.12): from raw chicory root to purified inulin
classification_rules.description: Inulin (a fructooligosaccharide — dietary fibre extracted from chicory) appears in its raw source and processed forms: RAW CHICORY ROOTS (Cichorium intybus var. sativum) grown for inulin — traded as agricultural commodity → 12.12 (chicory roots, fresh/dried/ground). INULIN EXTRACTED and purified from chicory root → 11.08 (heading 11.08.20 — inulin). The extraction and purification process moves the product from Ch.12 to Ch.11. Inulin SYRUP (inulin dissolved in water) → 11.08 or 17.02 depending on concentration and sucrose content.

classification_rules.rule: Vital wheat gluten (11.09) vs wheat starch (11.08) vs wheat flour (11.01): the separation process outcome
classification_rules.description: Wheat has three major separation products from milling: WHEAT FLOUR (11.01) = all components ground together (starch + gluten protein + fibre + minerals — whole wheat matrix unless refined). WHEAT STARCH (11.08.11) = starch extracted and separated from wheat, gluten removed by wet washing process (pure starch fraction). VITAL WHEAT GLUTEN (11.09) = protein fraction left after starch is washed away — approximately 75–85% protein, elastic, used as dough improver, meat analogue base. These three products come from the same raw material (wheat) but via different separation/processing routes — each gets its own heading. Denatured/cooked gluten (texturised wheat protein, seitan) → may leave Ch.11 for Ch.21 if food-prepared.
